name: Rape Crisis England & Wales
description: Specialist support for women and girls who have experienced sexual violence at any point in their life. ISVAs support you through police investigation and court.

name: SARSAS — Somerset & Avon Rape and Sexual Abuse Support
description: Free specialist counselling and ISVA support for those affected by rape and sexual abuse in Somerset and Avon. Example of a strong regional SARC-linked service.

name: National Domestic Abuse Helpline
description: Run by Refuge. Free, 24/7 helpline for women experiencing domestic abuse. Helps find local refuge spaces, safety planning, and specialist support.

name: Women's Aid
description: Live chat service, online community, and local service finder for women experiencing domestic abuse and coercive control. Survivors' handbook available online.

name: Refuge
description: Operates the National DA Helpline and a network of refuges across England for women and children fleeing domestic abuse.

name: Victim Support
description: Free support for victims and witnesses of any crime. Court support workers, emotional support, and help understanding the criminal justice system.

name: Revenge Porn Helpline
description: Specialist help removing intimate images shared without consent, including deepfakes. Works directly with platforms for fast removal.

name: StopNCII.org
description: Creates a digital fingerprint (hash) of your intimate image to prevent it being uploaded to major platforms. Free, confidential, and no image is stored.

name: Rights of Women
hours: Tue & Thu 7pm-9pm; Wed 12pm-2pm (sexual violence line: Tue 11am-1pm)
description: Free legal advice for women on domestic violence, sexual violence, family law and employment law. Helplines staffed by qualified solicitors and barristers.

name: NAPAC — National Association for People Abused in Childhood
description: Support for adult survivors of any type of childhood abuse, including sexual abuse. Helpline and online resources.

name: Galop — LGBT+ Anti-Violence Charity
description: Specialist support for LGBT+ people experiencing domestic abuse, sexual violence or hate crime. Understands the specific barriers LGBT+ people face.

name: SafeLives
description: Provides the DASH (Domestic Abuse, Stalking and Honour-based Violence) risk assessment tool used by police and services. Check your risk level online.

name: Citizens Advice
description: Free advice on legal options, housing rights, benefits, and signposting. Local branches across England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland.

name: Suzy Lamplugh Trust
description: National Stalking Helpline. Support and advice for those experiencing stalking. Also runs personal safety training.

name: NSPCC
description: If a child is at risk of sexual abuse or harm. Adults can call to report concerns. Childline (0800 1111) for children and young people.

name: Imkaan
description: UK-based Black and minority ethnic (BME) women's organisation working to address violence against BME women and girls. Can refer to specialist services.

name: Karma Nirvana
description: Support for victims of honour-based abuse and forced marriage across all communities and genders.
